_note: Illustrated editions of Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (and its 1864 manuscript ur-text). AI-assisted; biographical/edition details marked confidence:low must be web-verified before publishing. r2_folder = prefix under https://pub-71ebbc217e6247ecacb85126a6616699.r2.dev/grammar-illustrations/alice-in-wonderland/ where that edition's plates live.

editions.title: Alice's Adventures Under Ground
editions.note: Carroll's own handwritten, self-illustrated manuscript (~37 drawings), gifted to Alice Liddell. The ur-text that became Wonderland.

editions.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland (first edition)
editions.note: The canonical first edition: 42 wood-engraved Tenniel illustrations, Macmillan. 1865 print recalled over quality; 1866 dated reissue. Carroll directed Tenniel closely.

editions.title: The Nursery Alice (colorized Tenniel)
editions.note: Colorized Tenniel plates for younger readers. VERIFY colorist/printing attribution (E. Gertrude Thomson association vs. printer Edmund Evans).

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by W. H. Walker
editions.note: Part of the 1907 wave after UK copyright lapsed.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Arthur Rackham
editions.note: The most celebrated post-copyright edition (Heinemann): ~13 muted color plates + line drawings. Gutenberg #28885.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Brinsley Le Fanu
Alice — Tenniel re-colored by Harry Theaker
editions.note: Tenniel compositions re-colored by Theaker (1911). VERIFY.

editions.title: Alice — illustrated by Alice B. Woodward
Alice — illustrated by Bessie Pease Gutmann

_key_relationships: Carroll told the story to the Liddell sisters on a boat trip (4 July 1862); wrote it out as 'Under Ground' (1864) for Alice Liddell.
Macmillan published the expanded 'Wonderland' (1865) with Tenniel; Carroll paid/controlled production and famously micromanaged Tenniel.
UK copyright on the text lapsed in 1907 (Carroll d.1898) — triggering the 1907+ wave of new illustrated editions (Rackham, Walker, then Le Fanu, Woodward, Hudson, Rountree, Gutmann...).
Tenniel's 1865 designs remained the 'canonical' Alice that every later illustrator answered to.
